ranked_movies.title: Parasite
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently mentioned as a masterpiece and a key work in the debate between directors Bong Joon-ho and Park Chan-wook. It is consistently used as a benchmark for quality and is one of the most acclaimed modern films.

ranked_movies.title: Memories of Murder
ranked_movies.reason: Heavily discussed as a contender for the best film from either director. Many users argued it is so good that it single-handedly makes Bong Joon-ho's filmography worth keeping over Park's.

ranked_movies.title: Oldboy
ranked_movies.reason: Cited as one of the best films ever seen by multiple users. A cornerstone of Park Chan-wook's filmography and a major point in his favor during the director comparison.

ranked_movies.title: The Handmaiden
ranked_movies.reason: Often paired with 'Oldboy' as a top-tier Park Chan-wook film. Many users stated it is superior to 'Parasite', making it a critical title in the debate.

ranked_movies.title: Decision to Leave
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed alongside 'The Handmaiden' as a recent Park Chan-wook masterpiece that some users believe surpasses Bong Joon-ho's recent work.

ranked_movies.title: Save the Green Planet!
ranked_movies.reason: Explicitly mentioned as an underappreciated Korean movie that deserves more love. Its upcoming English-language remake, 'Bugonia', was also a major topic of box office discussion.

ranked_movies.title: The Chaser
ranked_movies.reason: Brought up in the context of 'The Yellow Sea' discussion. It is considered by some to be an even better, albeit harder to watch, film from director Na Hong-jin.

ranked_movies.title: The Yellow Sea
ranked_movies.reason: The central subject of a highly-upvoted post. Praised for its brutal action, score, and twisted story, sparking a wider conversation about underrated Korean films.

ranked_movies.title: Mother
ranked_movies.reason: Listed as one of Bong Joon-ho's top-notch films, placing it in the upper tier of his filmography during the comparison with Park.

ranked_movies.title: I Saw the Devil
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as an awesome movie from director Kim Jee-woon, brought into the discussion as another great Korean filmmaker alongside Bong and Park.

ranked_movies.title: Train to Busan
ranked_movies.reason: Indirectly referenced in a 'Star Trek' post title ('Shuttle to Kenfori'), confirming its status as a well-known cultural touchstone for Korean zombie films.

ranked_movies.title: A Dirty Carnival
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as an underappreciated Korean movie in the discussion sparked by 'The Yellow Sea'.

ranked_movies.title: Sea Fog
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as an underappreciated Korean movie in the discussion sparked by 'The Yellow Sea'.

ranked_movies.title: Welcome to Dongmakgol
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a potentially underappreciated Korean movie, though the commenter was unsure of its current reception.

ranked_movies.title: Asura: The City of Madness
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as an underappreciated Korean movie in the discussion sparked by 'The Yellow Sea'.

ranked_movies.title: Peppermint Candy
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a powerful, older Korean film that has stayed with viewers, mentioned in the 'underappreciated' thread.

ranked_movies.title: The Good, The Bad, The Weird
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as an awesome movie from director Kim Jee-woon, brought into the discussion as another great Korean filmmaker.

ranked_movies.title: Sympathy for Mr. Vengeance
ranked_movies.reason: Part of Park Chan-wook's celebrated filmography, contributing to his reputation for consistency and quality discussed in the director debate.

ranked_movies.title: Lady Vengeance
ranked_movies.reason: Part of Park Chan-wook's celebrated filmography, contributing to his reputation for consistency and quality discussed in the director debate.

ranked_movies.title: Okja
ranked_movies.reason: As part of Bong Joon-ho's filmography, it was part of the overall assessment of his work, though it was less highlighted than 'Parasite' or 'Memories of Murder' in the debates.
